chapter_summary: Jesus feeds 5,000 people with five loaves and two fish — a miracle so great the crowd wants to make Him king. That night He walks on water to His disciples' boat. The next day the crowd finds Him and He teaches that He is the Bread of Life — not just bread that fills the stomach for a day, but living bread from heaven that gives eternal life. Many followers turn back; Jesus asks the Twelve if they will leave too, and Peter says: 'Lord, to whom shall we go? You have the words of eternal life.'
main_lesson: Jesus is the Bread of Life — He is what our hearts are truly hungry for. Just as bread feeds our bodies, Jesus feeds our souls. When we come to Him and trust Him, He satisfies us with something that lasts forever.

memory_verse.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, I am the bread of life: he that cometh to me shall never hunger; and he that believeth on me shall never thirst.
memory_verse.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I am the Bread of Life. Whoever comes to Me will never be hungry again, and whoever trusts in Me will never be thirsty again.'

parent_guide: John 6 is one of the theological peaks of the Gospel of John. The feeding of the 5,000 (v1-15) is the only miracle recorded in all four gospels — its importance cannot be overstated. Jesus uses a boy's lunch, gives thanks, and creates abundance from almost nothing. The crowd's response (v15) — wanting to make Him king — shows they understood power but misunderstood mission. Walking on water (v16-21): the disciples are terrified; 'I AM' (ego eimi) is the divine name. The Bread of Life discourse (v22-71) is extended and demanding, moving from physical bread to spiritual bread to Jesus' body and blood (anticipating the Lord's Supper). For children: focus on the feeding (the boy's gift, the miracle, the satisfaction), the walking on water (Jesus comes to us in our fear), and the key verse v35 (Jesus satisfies our deepest hunger). The discourse can be simplified as: 'Jesus isn't just food for today — He's what your heart is hungry for forever.' Peter's confession (v68-69) is the anchor: 'You have the words of eternal life.'
application_for_children: A small boy gave Jesus his lunch — just five loaves and two fish — and Jesus used it to feed thousands! Jesus can take the small things we offer Him and do something amazing. And when you are worried or afraid, remember: Jesus walks right through the storm to find you.

verses.kjv: After these things Jesus went over the sea of Galilee, which is the sea of Tiberias.
verses.little_bible: After this, Jesus crossed to the other side of the Sea of Galilee, which people also called the Sea of Tiberias.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus crossed over the big lake called the Sea of Galilee.
verses.meaning: John sets the scene by the sea — a wide open space where a great miracle is about to happen. Jesus is always moving toward the next moment of grace.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus crossed the sea.
verses.prayer: Lord Jesus, wherever You go, I want to follow You.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been to a big lake or the sea? What did it look like?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus kept moving to new places to help new people. How can our family be ready to follow where Jesus leads us?
verses.do_it_today: Look at a map or globe and find the Sea of Galilee. Think about Jesus walking there long ago.
verses.illustration_prompt: A sparkling blue lake under a golden sky, with gentle hills in the background and a small wooden boat on the shore. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And a great multitude followed him, because they saw his miracles which he did on them that were diseased.
verses.little_bible: A huge crowd followed Jesus everywhere He went, because they had seen Him heal sick people and do amazing miracles.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A big crowd followed Jesus because they saw Him heal sick people.
verses.meaning: The crowd followed because of what Jesus did — but Jesus always wanted people to follow because of who He is. Signs point to the Saviour.
verses.memory_phrase: The crowd followed Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me follow You not just for what You give me, but because I love You.
verses.discussion_question: Why did all those people want to follow Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Is there a difference between following Jesus because of miracles and following Him because you love Him? What does our family follow Jesus for?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone one reason you love Jesus — not just what He has done, but who He is.
verses.illustration_prompt: A winding hillside path with a great crowd of men, women, and children in colourful robes following a figure in white ahead of them toward a distant green hillside. Bright watercolour.

verses.kjv: And Jesus went up into a mountain, and there he sat with his disciples.
verses.little_bible: Jesus went up a hillside and sat down with His disciples — His closest friends and followers.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went up a hill and sat with His friends.
verses.meaning: Jesus often went up high places to teach and to pray. Sitting down was a teacher's posture — He was about to do something important.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus sat with His friends.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want to sit close to You today and listen.
verses.discussion_question: Do you have a favourite place where you like to sit and be quiet?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus often went to quiet places before big events. How does your family make space to be still before God?
verses.do_it_today: Find a peaceful spot today. Sit quietly for one minute and think about Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus sitting peacefully on a green hillside, His disciples gathered closely around Him, the blue sea shimmering below in the distance. Gentle afternoon light, watercolour.

verses.kjv: And the passover, a feast of the Jews, was nigh.
verses.little_bible: It was almost time for the Passover, the special feast when God's people remembered how He rescued them from Egypt long ago.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: It was almost time for the big Jewish feast called Passover.
verses.meaning: John places the feeding miracle near Passover deliberately — Jesus, the true Passover Lamb and true bread, is about to feed His people in the wilderness just as God did with manna.
verses.memory_phrase: Passover was coming.
verses.prayer: Thank You, God, for remembering Your people. Thank You for Jesus, our true rescue.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a special meal your family celebrates together?
verses.family_discussion: Passover remembered how God saved His people through a lamb. How did Jesus fulfil what Passover pointed to?
verses.do_it_today: At your next meal together, thank God for one way He has rescued or helped your family.
verses.illustration_prompt: A warm family table set with candles and bread and wine, surrounded by happy faces, celebrating together in a stone room with hanging olive-branch decorations. Watercolour style.

verses.kjv: When Jesus then lifted up his eyes, and saw a great company come unto him, he saith unto Philip, Whence shall we buy bread, that these may eat?
verses.little_bible: Jesus looked up and saw a huge crowd coming toward Him. He asked Philip, 'Where can we buy enough bread to feed all these people?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus saw the big crowd and asked Philip, 'Where can we buy bread for everyone?'
verses.meaning: Jesus already knew what He was going to do — He asked the question to teach Philip (and us) to look to Him when a need seems impossible.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus saw the need.
verses.prayer: Lord, when I see a big problem, help me look to You first.
verses.discussion_question: What would you do if you had to feed thousands of people with almost no food?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus asked Philip a question He already knew the answer to. Why does God sometimes let us feel the weight of a problem before He solves it?
verses.do_it_today: Think of something that seems impossible. Pray and tell Jesus about it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing on a hillside, shading His eyes with His hand, looking out at a vast crowd streaming across the fields. Philip stands beside Him looking worried. Bright sky, watercolour.

verses.kjv: And this he said to prove him: for he himself knew what he would do.
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked this question to test Philip — because Jesus already had a plan. He always knows what He is going to do.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus was testing Philip. Jesus already knew His plan.
verses.meaning: Jesus never asks questions because He is confused — He asks to grow our faith. He had a miracle ready before the problem was even spoken aloud.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus always has a plan.
verses.prayer: Jesus, even when I don't know what to do, help me trust that You already have a plan.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been tested on something at school? Did you study to be ready?
verses.family_discussion: God sometimes lets us face big challenges as a test of trust, not because He has forgotten us. Can you think of a time God had a plan you couldn't see at first?
verses.do_it_today: Write or draw one thing that seems too hard right now. Below it, write: 'Jesus has a plan.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with a calm, knowing smile looking at a puzzled Philip, the crowd visible in the background, warm golden hills stretching behind them. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Philip answered him, Two hundred pennyworth of bread is not sufficient for them, that every one of them may take a little.
verses.little_bible: Philip answered, 'It would cost a huge amount of money — and even then it still wouldn't be enough to give everyone just a little bit!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Philip said, 'We don't have nearly enough money to buy that much bread!'
verses.meaning: Philip calculated the problem with human math and came up empty. God's math works differently — He starts where our resources end.
verses.memory_phrase: Not enough — but God is enough.
verses.prayer: Lord, when I feel like I don't have enough, remind me that You are more than enough.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever felt like you didn't have enough of something you needed?
verses.family_discussion: Philip looked at the money and saw 'not enough.' What happens in our family when we focus on what we don't have instead of on what God can do?
verses.do_it_today: Name three things God has already provided for you. Thank Him for each one.
verses.illustration_prompt: Philip with an open, empty money pouch in his hand, looking helplessly at the vast crowd on the hillside, his expression anxious. Bright daylight, warm colours, watercolour.

verses.kjv: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, saith unto him,
verses.little_bible: But one of Jesus' disciples — Andrew, who was Simon Peter's brother — stepped up and spoke.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Then Andrew, Peter's brother, spoke up.
verses.meaning: Andrew is often found in the Gospels bringing someone to Jesus. Here he is about to do it again — this time bringing a boy.
verses.memory_phrase: Andrew brought someone to Jesus.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me be like Andrew — always bringing people and things to Jesus.
verses.discussion_question: Who is someone you could introduce to Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Andrew's gift was connecting people to Jesus. How can each person in your family help others get to know Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for one friend by name and ask God to draw them closer to Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: Andrew stepping forward eagerly among the disciples, his face hopeful, his hand gesturing as if about to present something. The crowd is visible behind him on the hillside. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: There is a lad here, which hath five barley loaves, and two small fishes: but what are they among so many?
verses.little_bible: Andrew said, 'There is a young boy here who has five small loaves of bread and two little fish. But what is that for so many thousands of people?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Andrew found a boy with five small loaves and two little fish. But how could that feed so many?
verses.meaning: A boy's lunch — probably his whole meal for the day — is offered. It looks laughably small. But in Jesus' hands, the smallest gift becomes extraordinary.
verses.memory_phrase: A small gift, a big miracle.
verses.prayer: Jesus, take everything I have — even if it seems small — and use it for something amazing.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever shared something small with someone? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: The boy gave all he had. He didn't hold back even though it wasn't much. What does that teach us about generosity and trust?
verses.do_it_today: Give something away today — even something small. A kind word, a snack, a hug.
verses.illustration_prompt: A young boy with bright, curious eyes holding out a small woven basket with five round flatbreads and two tiny fish, looking up at Andrew and Jesus with a willing smile. Golden afternoon light, tender watercolour.

verses.kjv: And Jesus said, Make the men sit down. Now there was much grass in the place. So the men sat down, in number about five thousand.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Tell everyone to sit down.' There was plenty of green grass. So the people sat — about five thousand of them!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Make everyone sit down.' Five thousand people sat on the green grass.
verses.meaning: Jesus is calm and orderly in the face of an impossible problem. He prepared the people to receive — a lesson that God's provision comes when we are ready and settled.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus organised the miracle.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me be still and ready to receive what You want to give me.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever sat in a big crowd of people? How big was the crowd?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus brought order before the miracle. Why do you think it matters to be still and ready when God is about to do something?
verses.do_it_today: Before eating dinner, have everyone sit and be still for thirty seconds and thank Jesus together.
verses.illustration_prompt: Thousands of people settling onto bright green grass on a hillside in orderly groups, the late afternoon sun casting long golden shadows across the scene. Joyful, peaceful watercolour.

verses.kjv: And Jesus took the loaves; and when he had given thanks, he distributed to the disciples, and the disciples to them that were seated; and likewise of the fishes as much as they would.
verses.little_bible: Jesus took the loaves of bread, gave thanks to God, and then broke them and passed them out. He did the same with the fish. Everyone ate as much as they wanted!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said thank You to God and gave everyone bread and fish. Everyone had enough!
verses.meaning: Jesus gave thanks before the miracle — teaching us that gratitude comes before abundance. He involved His disciples in distributing, showing that His provision flows through ordinary people.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus gave thanks, then gave.
verses.prayer: Thank You, Jesus, for always giving me more than I need. Help me always give thanks before I eat.
verses.discussion_question: Do you say thank you before your meals? Who do you thank?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus gave thanks before the miracle happened. How does gratitude change the way we see what God provides?
verses.do_it_today: Say a heartfelt thank-you prayer before every meal today — and really mean it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus holding bread aloft with eyes lifted to heaven in thanksgiving, golden light streaming down, disciples lined up eagerly to carry baskets of bread and fish to the seated thousands. Joyful watercolour.

verses.kjv: When they were filled, he said unto his disciples, Gather up the fragments that remain, that nothing be lost.
verses.little_bible: After everyone had eaten until they were full, Jesus told His disciples, 'Now gather up all the leftovers — don't let anything go to waste.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: After everyone ate, Jesus said, 'Collect all the leftovers — don't waste any.'
verses.meaning: God's provision is generous — and He cares about stewardship. 'Let nothing be lost' echoes John 6:39, where Jesus says He will lose none of those the Father gives Him.
verses.memory_phrase: Let nothing be lost.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me value what You give me and never waste Your good gifts.
verses.discussion_question: What do you do with leftover food at home?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said not to waste the leftovers. What does it mean to be a good steward of what God provides — whether food, money, or time?
verses.do_it_today: Look around your home today and find something you have wasted or ignored. Take care of it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Disciples moving through rows of seated people, collecting pieces of bread and fish into woven baskets, the people looking full and content. Warm evening light, peaceful watercolour.

verses.kjv: Therefore they gathered them together, and filled twelve baskets with the fragments of the five barley loaves, which remained over and above unto them that had eaten.
verses.little_bible: They gathered all the pieces and filled twelve whole baskets — all from the five small loaves of bread that the boy had shared!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They filled twelve big baskets with leftovers from just five small loaves!
verses.meaning: Twelve baskets for twelve disciples — everyone had more than enough to carry away. God's abundance always exceeds what we bring to Him.
verses.memory_phrase: Twelve baskets full!
verses.prayer: God, You always give more than enough. Thank You for Your overflowing goodness.
verses.discussion_question: If you started with five pieces of bread and ended with twelve baskets full, how would you feel?
verses.family_discussion: The miracle produced more than when it started. Can you think of a time when God gave your family more than you expected?
verses.do_it_today: Draw or describe a time when God gave your family 'more than enough.' Share it with someone.
verses.illustration_prompt: Twelve large woven baskets overflowing with chunks of bread and fish, stacked in a row on green grass, the sun setting in warm oranges and golds behind them. Abundant, joyful watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then those men, when they had seen the miracle that Jesus did, said, This is of a truth that prophet that should come into the world.
verses.little_bible: When the people saw this miracle, they said, 'This must truly be the Prophet who God promised would come into the world!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The people saw the miracle and said, 'Jesus must be the special Prophet God promised!'
verses.meaning: The crowd recognised something extraordinary had happened — but they interpreted it through their political hopes rather than God's true purpose.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is the promised One.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are everything God promised. Help me recognise You for who You truly are.
verses.discussion_question: If you saw someone do something that seemed impossible, what would you think about them?
verses.family_discussion: The people were right that Jesus was special but wrong about what He came to do. How do we make sure we understand Jesus correctly today?
verses.do_it_today: Read one verse about who Jesus is (try John 1:1 or John 14:6) and talk about it with your family.
verses.illustration_prompt: Amazed people pointing and talking excitedly, mouths open, eyes wide with wonder, the hillside buzzing with excited conversation as the evening sky glows. Vibrant watercolour.

verses.kjv: When Jesus therefore perceived that they would come and take him by force, to make him a king, he departed again into a mountain himself alone.
verses.little_bible: Jesus knew the crowd wanted to grab Him and make Him their king by force. So He slipped away by Himself up into the hills.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The crowd wanted to make Jesus a king by force. So Jesus went away alone into the hills.
verses.meaning: Jesus refused a crown offered by human hands — because His kingdom is not won by force. He came to be a Saviour before a king, and to give His life rather than take power.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus chose God's way, not the crowd's way.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me follow Your way even when the world wants something different from You.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus walked away when they wanted to make Him king?
verses.family_discussion: The crowd wanted to force Jesus into a role He hadn't come to play yet. How do we sometimes try to make Jesus do what we want instead of following what He wants?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one area where you want God to do things your way. Pray and surrender it to His way.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus walking quietly alone up a rocky hillside path as the evening light fades, the crowd far below on the green plain, the solitary figure small against the vast sky. Peaceful watercolour.

verses.kjv: And when even was now come, his disciples went down unto the sea,
verses.little_bible: As evening came, Jesus' disciples went down to the shore of the sea.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When evening came, the disciples went down to the lake.
verses.meaning: Night is falling, Jesus is absent, and the disciples are heading to the water — the scene is being set for a moment of fear and divine rescue.
verses.memory_phrase: Evening came; the disciples went to the sea.
verses.prayer: Lord, even when evening comes and I can't see You, I know You are near.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been somewhere at night without someone you trusted? How did it feel?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples went ahead without Jesus. Are there times our family moves forward on our own without asking Jesus to lead us?
verses.do_it_today: Before bed tonight, take a moment to invite Jesus into the rest of your evening.
verses.illustration_prompt: Disciples walking down a rocky hillside path toward a dark shimmering sea at dusk, the last light of the sun reflecting gold on the water, their figures small and quiet. Serene watercolour.

verses.kjv: And entered into a ship, and went over the sea toward Capernaum. And it was now dark, and Jesus was not come to them.
verses.little_bible: They got into a boat and started rowing toward Capernaum. It was now completely dark, and Jesus had not yet come to them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They got in a boat in the dark. Jesus had not come yet.
verses.meaning: Darkness and absence — two of the most vulnerable feelings anyone can have. This is the human experience into which Jesus is about to walk on water.
verses.memory_phrase: Dark and alone — but not for long.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when I feel alone in the dark, remind me You are coming.
verses.discussion_question: What helps you feel safe when it is dark and you are afraid?
verses.family_discussion: There are seasons in life when it is dark and Jesus seems far away. What has helped your family trust God in those times?
verses.do_it_today: If you know someone going through a 'dark' time, send them a message reminding them Jesus is near.
verses.illustration_prompt: A small wooden boat on a dark sea at night, four disciples rowing hard, their faces lit by a single lantern, the sea stretching into deep blue-black shadows around them. Atmospheric watercolour.

verses.kjv: And the sea arose by reason of a great wind that blew.
verses.little_bible: Then a strong wind began to blow and the sea became rough and stormy.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A big wind came and made the waves rough and wild.
verses.meaning: The storm did not catch Jesus by surprise — it was part of the moment in which He would reveal His power and His care for His frightened disciples.
verses.memory_phrase: A storm rose on the sea.
verses.prayer: Lord, You are stronger than any storm. Help me trust You when life gets rough.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been in a really big storm? What was it like?
verses.family_discussion: Storms in the Bible often happen right before God shows up powerfully. Can you think of a 'storm' in your life that God turned into a story of His faithfulness?
verses.do_it_today: Find a picture of a stormy sea. Think about how big and scary it looks — and then remember: Jesus is bigger.
verses.illustration_prompt: A small wooden boat tossed on dark churning waves, sails straining in the wind, lightning flashing in stormy clouds above, the disciples gripping the sides in fear. Dramatic watercolour.

verses.kjv: So when they had rowed about five and twenty or thirty furlongs, they see Jesus walking on the sea, and drawing nigh unto the ship: and they were afraid.
verses.little_bible: After they had rowed about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on top of the water toward their boat — and they were terrified!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They saw Jesus walking on the water toward them and they were scared!
verses.meaning: Only God can walk on the sea in Hebrew understanding (Job 9:8). The disciples' terror makes sense — they are witnessing divinity up close.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus walked on the water.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You walk on water! Nothing is too hard for You. Help me trust You completely.
verses.discussion_question: How would you feel if you saw someone walking on top of the water?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus walked on the water — breaking every natural law — to get to His disciples. What does that tell us about how far Jesus will go to reach us?
verses.do_it_today: Tell a younger child the story of Jesus walking on water in your own words.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus walking calmly on the dark churning sea, light emanating from Him, the small boat visible ahead with wide-eyed terrified disciples inside. Miraculous, glowing watercolour.

verses.kjv: But he saith unto them, It is I; be not afraid.
verses.little_bible: But Jesus called out to them: 'It is I — do not be afraid!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'It's Me! Don't be afraid!'
verses.meaning: 'It is I' (Greek: ego eimi) is the divine name — 'I AM.' Jesus is telling them and us: God Himself has come. With God present, fear has no place.
verses.memory_phrase: It is I — do not be afraid!
verses.prayer: Jesus, when I am afraid, let me hear Your voice saying, 'It is I — do not be afraid.'
verses.discussion_question: What is something you are afraid of? What would you feel if Jesus said, 'Do not be afraid' right to you?
verses.family_discussion: 'It is I' is the same name God gave Moses at the burning bush. What does it mean that Jesus uses God's name? How should that affect our fear?
verses.do_it_today: Write 'Do not be afraid' on a piece of paper and put it somewhere you will see it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing on the water, arms slightly outstretched, His face calm and kind, light radiating from Him as He calls out to the terrified disciples in the boat nearby. Peaceful and powerful watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then they willingly received him into the ship: and immediately the ship was at the land whither they went.
verses.little_bible: Gladly they pulled Jesus into the boat — and instantly the boat was at the shore where they were heading!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They gladly let Jesus in the boat, and right away they reached land.
verses.meaning: When Jesus enters the situation, the journey is instantly complete. His presence doesn't just calm the storm — it brings you home.
verses.memory_phrase: When Jesus comes in, you arrive home.
verses.prayer: Jesus, come into my life fully. When You are with me, I know I will reach where I need to go.
verses.discussion_question: When you feel scared and someone you love comes to help you, how does that make you feel?
verses.family_discussion: The boat arrived immediately once Jesus was in it. How has inviting Jesus into a situation in your family brought peace or resolution faster than you expected?
verses.do_it_today: Is there a situation in your life where you haven't fully invited Jesus in? Pray and invite Him now.
verses.illustration_prompt: The disciples joyfully helping Jesus into the boat, wide smiles replacing their previous fear, the distant shoreline now suddenly close and welcoming with soft golden lights. Joyful watercolour.

verses.kjv: The day following, when the people which stood on the other side of the sea saw that there was none other boat there, save that one whereinto his disciples were entered, and that Jesus went not with his disciples into the boat, but that his disciples were gone away alone;
verses.little_bible: The next morning, the crowd still on the other shore noticed there had been only one boat — and that Jesus had not got into it with His disciples. They had left without Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The next day the crowd saw that Jesus had not left on the boat with the disciples.
verses.meaning: The crowd is puzzled — and their puzzlement drives them to seek Jesus further. Even confusion can become a reason to search for Christ.
verses.memory_phrase: The crowd searched for Jesus.
verses.prayer: Lord, when I don't understand, help me search for You rather than give up.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever looked everywhere for something you really wanted to find?
verses.family_discussion: The crowd's confusion led them to seek Jesus. How can questions and confusion about faith actually lead us closer to God rather than away?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one question you have about God or faith. Ask a parent, pastor, or look for the answer in the Bible.
verses.illustration_prompt: Confused people on a lakeside shore pointing at the empty spot where the boat had been, looking around wondering where Jesus went. Morning light on calm water, gentle watercolour.

verses.kjv: (Howbeit there came other boats from Tiberias nigh unto the place where they did eat bread, after that the Lord had given thanks:)
verses.little_bible: (However, other boats had come from Tiberias and arrived close to where Jesus had given thanks and fed everyone.)
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Other boats had arrived near the place where Jesus had fed the crowd.
verses.meaning: John carefully notes that this place was where the Lord had given thanks — anchoring the whole journey back to the miracle and the act of gratitude.
verses.memory_phrase: Where Jesus gave thanks.
verses.prayer: Lord, let my life be marked by the places where I gave thanks to You.
verses.discussion_question: Do you have a special place where something amazing happened with your family?
verses.family_discussion: John remembered the exact detail of where Jesus gave thanks. What moments in your family's faith story do you never want to forget?
verses.do_it_today: Share one memory of God's goodness around your dinner table tonight.
verses.illustration_prompt: Wooden fishing boats arriving at a peaceful lakeside, the sun rising over the hills where the grass still shows the marks of thousands of people having sat in rows. Serene morning watercolour.

verses.kjv: When the people therefore saw that Jesus was not there, neither his disciples, they also took shipping, and came to Capernaum, seeking for Jesus.
verses.little_bible: When the crowd realised Jesus and the disciples were both gone, they got into boats and sailed to Capernaum — looking for Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When they could not find Jesus, they got into boats and went to look for Him.
verses.meaning: They sought Jesus — but as the next verses will show, they were seeking the wrong thing from Him. Seeking Jesus for the right reason matters.
verses.memory_phrase: They went looking for Jesus.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me seek You for the right reasons — not just for what You give, but for who You are.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you seek Jesus? What do you look for from Him?
verses.family_discussion: The crowd looked for Jesus — but verse 26 reveals their motives weren't pure. How do we examine our own hearts about why we come to God?
verses.do_it_today: Spend two minutes simply telling Jesus you love Him — before asking for anything.
verses.illustration_prompt: People climbing eagerly into fishing boats on a bright morning sea, pointing toward the horizon, determined to find Jesus. Clear blue water, hopeful watercolour.

verses.kjv: And when they had found him on the other side of the sea, they said unto him, Rabbi, when camest thou hither?
verses.little_bible: They found Jesus on the other side of the sea and asked, 'Teacher, when did You get here?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They found Jesus and asked, 'Teacher, when did You get here?'
verses.meaning: They called Him 'Rabbi' — teacher. They were focused on the how and when, not on who He truly was. Jesus will redirect their question entirely.
verses.memory_phrase: They found Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want to find You today — not just answers, but You Yourself.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been surprised to find someone somewhere you didn't expect them?
verses.family_discussion: The crowd was curious about how Jesus got there, not about who He was. Is it possible to know a lot of facts about Jesus without truly knowing Him?
verses.do_it_today: Spend time today reading about who Jesus is — not just what He did, but who He is.
verses.illustration_prompt: The crowd pressing around Jesus at a lakeside in Capernaum, eager faces asking questions, Jesus standing calmly in a colonnaded space near the water. Warm bright watercolour.

verses.kjv: Jesus answered them and said, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Ye seek me, not because ye saw the miracles, but because ye did eat of the loaves, and were filled.
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered them: 'I tell you the truth — you are looking for Me not because you understood the miracle, but because you ate the bread and had a full stomach!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'You're looking for Me because you got a free meal, not because you understand who I am!'
verses.meaning: Jesus cuts through surface-level following with piercing honesty. He knows the difference between those who follow for the gift and those who follow for the Giver.
verses.memory_phrase: Follow the Giver, not just the gift.
verses.prayer: Jesus, forgive me when I seek You only for what I can get. Help me seek You for who You are.
verses.discussion_question: Would you be friends with someone just because they gave you things? What makes a real friendship?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus sees the motives behind our actions. How honest can we be about why we come to God? What are we really looking for?
verses.do_it_today: Write down three reasons you love Jesus that have nothing to do with what He gives you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking directly and lovingly to the crowd with an open hand, His gaze kind but piercing, the people listening with mixed expressions — some surprised, some convicted. Warm watercolour.

verses.kjv: Labour not for the meat which perisheth, but for that meat which endureth unto everlasting life, which the Son of man shall give unto you: for him hath God the Father sealed.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Don't work so hard for food that only lasts a day. Work for the food that gives you life forever — that is what I, the Son of Man, will give you. God the Father has marked Me as the one to give it.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Don't just work for food that goes bad. Work for the food that lasts forever — I will give it to you!'
verses.meaning: Jesus inverts earthly priorities — what the world calls work (earning food) He calls unnecessary; what He calls essential (receiving eternal life) is a gift, not something we earn.
verses.memory_phrase: Work for food that lasts forever.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me put first things first — seeking You and Your eternal life above all else.
verses.discussion_question: What things do you work hardest for? Which of those things will last forever?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus is saying the most important thing in life is not earning money for food but receiving eternal life. How does that change what we prioritise as a family?
verses.do_it_today: As a family, list what you 'work for' each week. Pray together to make eternal things the priority.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gesturing upward with one hand while the crowd listens intently, bread and fish visible in the foreground beginning to wither, while above radiates a golden heavenly light. Symbolic watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then said they unto him, What shall we do, that we might work the works of God?
verses.little_bible: The people asked Him, 'What do we need to do to do the work God wants?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They asked Jesus, 'What do we need to do to please God?'
verses.meaning: They thought eternal life was another task to accomplish — another work to perform. Jesus is about to give the most surprising answer: it is not a work, it is a gift.
verses.memory_phrase: What must we do?
verses.prayer: Lord, thank You that Your gift of life is not earned. Help me receive it with grateful hands.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever thought you had to do something special to make God love you?
verses.family_discussion: Many people think being good enough earns God's approval. What does the Bible say about how we actually receive eternal life?
verses.do_it_today: Read Romans 6:23 together — 'the gift of God is eternal life.' Talk about what a gift means.
verses.illustration_prompt: People in the crowd leaning forward earnestly, hands raised asking a question, their faces sincere and searching. Jesus listening attentively with a calm, knowing expression. Warm watercolour.

verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto them, This is the work of God, that ye believe on him whom he hath sent.
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered them: 'The work God wants is this — believe in the One God has sent.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'The one thing God asks is this: believe in Me, the One He sent.'
verses.meaning: The entire 'work' of salvation collapses into one thing: faith. Belief in Jesus is not passive — it is the greatest and most life-changing action a person can take.
verses.memory_phrase: Believe in the One God sent.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe in You. You are the One God sent to save me. I trust You with my whole life.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to believe in someone — not just believe they exist, but really trust them?
verses.family_discussion: Believing in Jesus is the one 'work' that opens eternal life. How do we make sure our children understand this is about trusting Jesus, not about being religious?
verses.do_it_today: Say out loud: 'I believe in Jesus, the One God sent.' Mean it with your whole heart.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus holding out His hand toward the crowd, His expression warm and inviting, golden light radiating around His head, the crowd looking at Him with dawning understanding. Gentle watercolour.

verses.kjv: They said therefore unto him, What sign shewest thou then, that we may see, and believe thee? what dost thou work?
verses.little_bible: So they said to Him, 'What miracle can You do so we can see it and believe You? What amazing thing will You do?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They said, 'What miracle will You do so we can believe You?'
verses.meaning: This is breathtaking — they just saw Him feed 5,000 people but demand another sign. Our hearts can always find a reason to delay belief if we aren't ready to surrender.
verses.memory_phrase: They wanted another sign.
verses.prayer: Lord, forgive me when I keep asking for more proof instead of trusting what I already know about You.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen something amazing but still weren't sure you believed it? What made you finally believe?
verses.family_discussion: The crowd had just witnessed a miracle but wanted another one. Why is it dangerous to make our faith depend on signs rather than on trusting who Jesus is?
verses.do_it_today: Count three things you already know about Jesus that are worth trusting. Write them down.
verses.illustration_prompt: Crowd members with arms crossed and demanding expressions, some pointing at Jesus expectantly. Jesus standing quietly confident among them, unmoved by the demand. Tension shown in warm colours.

verses.kjv: Our fathers did eat manna in the desert; as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat.
verses.little_bible: They said, 'Moses gave our ancestors bread from heaven in the desert — it's written in Scripture: He gave them bread from heaven to eat.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They said, 'Moses gave our ancestors bread from heaven in the desert!'
verses.meaning: They pointed to manna as if to set the bar for Jesus — 'Can You match Moses?' Jesus will exceed Moses by being the bread Himself, not just providing it.
verses.memory_phrase: God gave them bread from heaven.
verses.prayer: Lord, thank You for providing what Your people needed throughout all of history. You never stop providing.
verses.discussion_question: Have you heard the story of manna in the desert? What was amazing about it?
verses.family_discussion: The crowd compared Jesus to Moses. But how is Jesus greater than Moses and greater than manna?
verses.do_it_today: Read Exodus 16:4 about manna and talk about how God provides for His people every day.
verses.illustration_prompt: A desert scene with small white flakes drifting down from a bright sky onto the outstretched hands of ancient Israelites in robes, their faces lifted in wonder and relief. Warm, golden watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Moses gave you not that bread from heaven; but my Father giveth you the true bread from heaven.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told them: 'I tell you truly — it was not Moses who gave you bread from heaven. It is My Father who gives you the real bread from heaven!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'It wasn't Moses who gave the bread. My Father gives the true bread from heaven!'
verses.meaning: Jesus corrects their history and elevates their hope: Moses was the instrument, but God was always the provider — and now God gives something far better than manna.
verses.memory_phrase: My Father gives the true bread.
verses.prayer: Father God, thank You for giving us something better than manna — You gave us Jesus.
verses.discussion_question: What is better — bread that only lasts a day, or bread that gives life forever?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus corrected a misunderstanding about their history. Are there things about God or Jesus that you used to believe that you've come to understand better?
verses.do_it_today: Thank God today for giving something better than what you expected — give an example from your own life.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gesturing upward with His finger pointing to heaven, His expression firm and clear, the crowd listening with expressions of surprise and dawning curiosity. Bright sky, blue and gold watercolour.

verses.kjv: For the bread of God is he which cometh down from heaven, and giveth life unto the world.
verses.little_bible: Jesus explained: 'The bread God gives is the One who comes down from heaven and gives life to the whole world.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The bread of God comes from heaven and gives life to the whole world.
verses.meaning: Jesus is describing Himself without quite saying it yet — He is the heavenly bread, come down to give life not just to Israel but to the whole world.
verses.memory_phrase: Bread from heaven gives life to the world.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You came from heaven to give life to the whole world — including me. Thank You.
verses.discussion_question: If a bread could give life to everyone in the world, how precious would that bread be?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus came not just for one nation but to give life to the whole world. How does that shape the way we think about people who are different from us?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for someone from a different country or culture today — God loves them as much as He loves you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A glowing loaf of bread descending from a bright heaven through clouds toward a diverse crowd of people reaching up from earth below, all races and ages represented. Beautiful symbolic watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then said they unto him, Lord, evermore give us this bread.
verses.little_bible: The people said, 'Lord, please give us this bread every single day, for ever!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They said, 'Lord, please give us this bread always!'
verses.meaning: Like the woman at the well asking for living water, the crowd is beginning to want what Jesus is offering — though they still think of it in physical terms. Desire is the doorway.
verses.memory_phrase: Give us this bread always!
verses.prayer: Lord, give me a heart that always wants more of You — more than anything else.
verses.discussion_question: Is there something you wish you could have every single day forever?
verses.family_discussion: The crowd asked for the bread without yet understanding it was Jesus Himself. How do our longings — even shallow ones — sometimes point us toward deeper spiritual needs?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God today: 'Give me more of You, Lord.' Say it simply and mean it.
verses.illustration_prompt: The crowd reaching forward with upturned hands and hopeful, eager faces, their eyes fixed on Jesus who stands calmly before them in warm light. A moment of longing and invitation. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, I am the bread of life: he that cometh to me shall never hunger; and he that believeth on me shall never thirst.
verses.little_bible: And Jesus said: 'I AM the Bread of Life! Whoever comes to Me will never be hungry again — ever. And whoever trusts in Me will never be thirsty again. I am all you need!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'I am the Bread of Life! Come to Me and you will never be hungry or thirsty again.'
verses.meaning: The greatest 'I AM' declaration in John 6. Jesus does not give bread — He IS the bread. Coming to Him and believing in Him are the two movements of saving faith.
verses.memory_phrase: I AM the Bread of Life.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are the Bread of Life! I come to You and I trust in You. Fill me with what only You can give.
verses.discussion_question: What does it feel like to be really, truly hungry? Now imagine never being hungry in your soul again — what would that feel like?
verses.family_discussion: 'I AM' is the name of God from Exodus 3. Why is it so significant that Jesus uses these words? What is He claiming about Himself?
verses.do_it_today: Memorise John 6:35 together as a family. Write it on a card and put it on the fridge.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing tall with arms open wide, a warm golden radiance surrounding Him, the crowd before Him with eyes wide and mouths slightly open in awe. Bread imagery subtly woven in glowing patterns around His figure. Majestic, tender watercolour.

verses.kjv: But I said unto you, That ye also have seen me, and believe not.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'But I have told you — you have seen Me with your own eyes and you still don't believe.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'You have seen Me, but you still do not believe.'
verses.meaning: Seeing is not enough — the heart must be willing to trust. The tragedy of unbelief is not ignorance but resistance.
verses.memory_phrase: Seeing is not enough without believing.
verses.prayer: Lord, don't let me be someone who sees You and still holds back. I choose to believe.
verses.discussion_question: Can you see something with your eyes and still not believe it? Has that ever happened to you?
verses.family_discussion: The crowd saw miracles but didn't truly believe. What is the difference between intellectual knowledge about Jesus and real saving faith?
verses.do_it_today: Examine your heart honestly: have you really trusted Jesus, or are you still watching from a distance?
verses.illustration_prompt: A few people at the edge of the crowd with their arms crossed and unconvinced expressions, while others lean forward in belief. Jesus looking at the unconvinced ones with patient, sorrowful love. Honest watercolour.

verses.kjv: All that the Father giveth me shall come to me; and him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Everyone the Father gives Me will come to Me — and I will never, ever turn away anyone who comes to Me. Never!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Everyone who comes to Me, I will never send away — not ever!'
verses.meaning: One of the most comforting promises in all of Scripture. No matter what you've done, no matter how many times you've failed, Jesus says: if you come to Me, I will not turn you away. Ever.
verses.memory_phrase: I will never turn you away.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that when I come to You, You never push me away. I am coming to You right now.
verses.discussion_question: How would you feel if you went to a friend's house and they slammed the door in your face? Now how does it feel to know Jesus will never do that?
verses.family_discussion: 'In no wise cast out' is one of the strongest promises in the Bible. Have you ever felt like God might reject you? What does this verse say to that feeling?
verses.do_it_today: If there's something in your life making you feel unworthy of God, bring it to Jesus today. He will not cast you out.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus at an open door with welcoming arms, warm light pouring from inside, a small child and a tired adult approaching up the path — Jesus smiling gently, arms open wide. Tender, welcoming watercolour.

verses.kjv: For I came down from heaven, not to do mine own will, but the will of him that sent me.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I did not come from heaven to do what I want — I came to do what My Father who sent Me wants.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'I came from heaven to do what My Father wants, not what I want.'
verses.meaning: Jesus' entire life is shaped by obedience to the Father. This is not weakness but perfect love — and it is the model for every follower of Christ.
verses.memory_phrase: I came to do My Father's will.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me want to do what God wants more than what I want — just like You did.
verses.discussion_question: Is it easy or hard to do what someone else wants instead of what you want? When is it worth it?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus voluntarily submitted His will to the Father. What does surrender to God look like in practical family life?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one thing you want to do today. Ask God if it lines up with what He wants — and be willing to change it if needed.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus looking upward with a serene and devoted expression, one hand on His heart, the sky above filled with soft golden light as if He is in communion with the Father. Peaceful watercolour.

verses.kjv: And this is the Father's will which hath sent me, that of all which he hath given me I should lose nothing, but should raise it up again at the last day.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'And this is what My Father who sent Me wants: that I keep safe every person He gives Me — not losing even one — and raise them all up on the last day.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'God wants Me to keep every person safe — and raise them up on the last day.'
verses.meaning: Jesus is the guardian of every soul the Father entrusts to Him. This is a promise of security — God does not lose His children.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus keeps every one safe.
verses.prayer: Thank You, Jesus, that You do not lose the people the Father gives You. I am safe in Your hands.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been in charge of keeping something safe and not losing it? How did that feel?
verses.family_discussion: God's will for Jesus was that He would not lose even one person. How does this promise give us security in our faith, especially during hard times?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for someone you love by name: 'Lord, keep [name] safe in Your hands.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with hands cupped gently around a group of tiny glowing figures — protecting them like precious treasure — under a vast, starry night sky. The Last Day hinted at by a distant dawn on the horizon. Tender watercolour.

verses.kjv: And this is the will of him that sent me, that every one which seeth the Son, and believeth on him, may have everlasting life: and I will raise him up at the last day.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'It is God's will that everyone who looks to the Son and believes in Him will have everlasting life — and I will raise that person up on the last day.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God wants everyone who believes in Jesus to have everlasting life. Jesus will raise them up on the last day.
verses.meaning: The gift is clear: see Jesus for who He is, trust in Him, receive eternal life. And the resurrection guarantee makes this the most secure promise possible.
verses.memory_phrase: Believe in the Son; receive eternal life.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I look to You and I believe in You. I receive the gift of everlasting life.
verses.discussion_question: What does 'everlasting life' mean to you? What do you think it will be like?
verses.family_discussion: God's will and our good are the same thing here. How does it feel to know God actively wants you to have eternal life?
verses.do_it_today: Tell a family member the good news: God wants everyone who believes in Jesus to have eternal life.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child looking up at a glowing figure of Jesus with wide, trusting eyes, and from above a gentle golden light surrounding them both, while in the distance a sunrise paints the horizon with new life. Hope-filled watercolour.

verses.kjv: The Jews then murmured at him, because he said, I am the bread which came down from heaven.
verses.little_bible: The Jewish religious leaders started grumbling about Jesus because He said, 'I am the bread that came down from heaven.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The religious leaders grumbled because Jesus said He came down from heaven.
verses.meaning: Grumbling is the language of unbelief. They were offended not by the claim being false, but by the claim being too big to fit their small picture of who Jesus could be.
verses.memory_phrase: Don't grumble — believe.
verses.prayer: Lord, when something You say is hard to understand, help me ask questions with a humble heart instead of grumbling.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever grumbled about something instead of asking a question? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: Grumbling is the opposite of faith. When our family is frustrated with God, what is a better response than complaining?
verses.do_it_today: Replace one grumble today with a genuine question to God — 'Lord, help me understand...'
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of religious leaders in long robes huddled together whispering and frowning, while Jesus stands apart looking at them with quiet patience. The crowd watching the tension. Honest watercolour.

verses.kjv: And they said, Is not this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? how is it then that he saith, I came down from heaven?
verses.little_bible: They said, 'Isn't this Jesus — Joseph's son? We know His father and mother! How can He say He came down from heaven?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They said, 'We know His parents! How can He say He came from heaven?'
verses.meaning: They knew the earthly side of Jesus but missed the divine. Sometimes familiarity can blind us to glory — we see the ordinary shell and miss the extraordinary reality.
verses.memory_phrase: They knew His family, but missed His glory.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me never let familiarity with the story of Jesus blind me to how extraordinary He truly is.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever thought you knew everything about someone — and then discovered something amazing about them?
verses.family_discussion: The people who knew Jesus' family were the most confused about who He was. How can we keep a fresh sense of wonder about Jesus even though we've heard about Him all our lives?
verses.do_it_today: Read one verse about Jesus today as if you've never heard it before. Let it be new to you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Religious leaders gesturing toward Jesus with raised eyebrows and confused expressions, pointing as if to say 'But we know this man!' Jesus remaining calm and serene. Warm community-setting watercolour.

verses.kjv: Jesus therefore answered and said unto them, Murmur not among yourselves.
verses.little_bible: Jesus replied: 'Stop grumbling among yourselves!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Stop grumbling among yourselves!'
verses.meaning: Jesus addresses the heart attitude first. Before teaching truth, He calls out the spirit in which they're receiving it. Grumbling closes the ears.
verses.memory_phrase: Stop grumbling; start listening.
verses.prayer: Lord, guard my heart against grumbling. Help me come to You with an open, ready spirit.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between grumbling and asking a genuine question?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus commanded them to stop grumbling before He taught them more. Why does our attitude matter when we come to hear from God?
verses.do_it_today: Notice every time you grumble today. Swap each grumble for a prayer.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus calmly raising a hand to quiet the murmuring crowd, His expression firm but gentle, the religious leaders looking slightly startled at being addressed so directly. Clear, honest watercolour.

verses.kjv: No man can come to me, except the Father which hath sent me draw him: and I will raise him up at the last day.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'No one can come to Me unless the Father who sent Me draws them to Me — and I will raise that person up on the last day.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'No one can come to Me unless the Father brings them — and I will raise them up.'
verses.meaning: Coming to Jesus is a work of grace from start to finish. Even our desire to seek God is a gift from God. This is humbling and glorious — salvation is entirely of God.
verses.memory_phrase: The Father draws us to Jesus.
verses.prayer: Father, thank You for drawing my heart toward Jesus. I didn't find You on my own — You found me.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever felt pulled toward Jesus — like He was drawing you closer? What was that like?
verses.family_discussion: If God draws people to Jesus, how does that change the way we pray for people who don't yet believe? What is our role and what is God's role?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for three people you love who haven't trusted Jesus yet: 'Father, draw them to Jesus.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A figure walking toward Jesus, a gentle golden thread of light connecting them, as if being lovingly pulled. The Father's hands unseen but suggested in the light above. Beautiful, grace-filled watercolour.

verses.kjv: It is written in the prophets, And they shall be all taught of God. Every man therefore that hath heard, and hath learned of the Father, cometh unto me.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'The prophets wrote: they shall all be taught by God. So everyone who has listened to the Father and learned from Him will come to Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Everyone who listens to God and learns from Him will come to Me.'
verses.meaning: God's teaching doesn't draw people away from Jesus — it leads directly to Him. Real knowledge of the Father always results in coming to the Son.
verses.memory_phrase: Taught by God, drawn to Jesus.
verses.prayer: Father, teach me. Let everything I learn about You draw me closer to Jesus.
verses.discussion_question: When a good teacher teaches you something, how does it change what you do?
verses.family_discussion: How does learning about God in our home and church draw our family closer to Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Read one short passage in the Bible today as a family. Ask: what does this teach us about God?
verses.illustration_prompt: An open scroll with ancient Hebrew text glowing with golden light, a child reading it intently, and ahead of them a path leading toward a figure of Jesus bathed in warm light. Connecting watercolour.

verses.kjv: Not that any man hath seen the Father, save he which is of God, he hath seen the Father.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'No one has seen the Father except Me — the One who is from God. I have seen the Father.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Only I have truly seen the Father, because I am from God.'
verses.meaning: Jesus is the unique revealer of the Father. No one else has had the same closeness, the same eternal union. To see Jesus is to see the Father.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus alone has seen the Father.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You show me what the Father is like. When I look at You, I see God.
verses.discussion_question: If you wanted to know what someone was like, who would you ask — a stranger or their closest family member?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said no one has seen the Father except Him. How does Jesus show us what God is like? What do we learn about God from watching Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Read John 14:9 together: 'Anyone who has seen Me has seen the Father.' List three things about Jesus that show you what God is like.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing between earth and a luminous heavenly light, one hand pointing upward as if connecting two worlds, His face calm and authoritative. A bridge between heaven and earth. Ethereal watercolour.

verses.kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth on me hath everlasting life.
verses.little_bible: Jesus declared: 'I am telling you the absolute truth — whoever believes in Me has everlasting life. Right now. Already.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Whoever believes in Me has everlasting life — right now!'
verses.meaning: Eternal life is not just a future promise — it begins the moment we believe. Jesus says 'hath' — present tense. Trust in Jesus is the possession of eternal life right now.
verses.memory_phrase: Believe in Me — have eternal life.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe in You. Thank You that I have eternal life — right now, today.
verses.discussion_question: If you were given something wonderful as a gift, do you have to wait until later to enjoy it? When do you get to have it?
verses.family_discussion: Eternal life starts now. How should that change the way we live today — not just what we hope for after death?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone today: 'If you believe in Jesus, you have eternal life — starting right now.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A single person standing with hands open, eyes closed in trust, surrounded by a warm glowing light that radiates outward — the visible picture of 'having eternal life' in this very moment. Peaceful watercolour.

verses.kjv: I am the bread of life.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said simply and powerfully: 'I AM the Bread of Life.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'I AM the Bread of Life.'
verses.meaning: The shortest 'I AM' statement — pure and unembellished. Jesus repeats His greatest claim. In case anyone missed it: HE is the bread. Not bread from heaven — He IS the bread.
verses.memory_phrase: I AM the Bread of Life.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are the Bread of Life. I need You every single day.
verses.discussion_question: What do you need every day to stay alive and healthy? Now think — what does your soul need every day?
verses.family_discussion: This is the second time Jesus says 'I AM the Bread of Life.' Why do you think He repeated it? What does that tell us about how important this truth is?
verses.do_it_today: Say it out loud together: 'Jesus is the Bread of Life.' Say it three times and mean it each time.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing alone against a clear golden sky, arms slightly open, radiating quiet authority, a single glowing loaf of bread superimposed gently at His heart level. Bold and simple watercolour.

verses.kjv: Your fathers did eat manna in the wilderness, and are dead.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Your ancestors ate manna in the wilderness — and they still died.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Your ancestors ate manna in the desert — but they still died.'
verses.meaning: Manna was miraculous, but it was temporary. It kept people alive for a day. Jesus is offering what manna could never provide: life that doesn't end.
verses.memory_phrase: Manna was temporary. Jesus is eternal.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me not settle for temporary things when You offer me something eternal.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between something that lasts for a little while and something that lasts forever?
verses.family_discussion: Manna was a gift from God, but even it couldn't give eternal life. What are the 'good things' in our lives that are gifts from God but not the same as Jesus Himself?
verses.do_it_today: Name one good thing in your life. Then thank God that Jesus is even better than that good thing.
verses.illustration_prompt: A stark contrast image: ancient stone tombs in a desert with manna-shaped symbols, beside a glowing figure of Jesus representing eternal life. The contrast between death and life. Thoughtful watercolour.

verses.kjv: This is the bread which cometh down from heaven, that a man may eat thereof, and not die.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'But here is the bread that comes down from heaven — whoever eats it will never die.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'The bread from heaven lets whoever eats it live forever and never die.'
verses.meaning: Jesus is the answer to every person's deepest fear: death. He came from heaven to offer life without end to all who receive Him.
verses.memory_phrase: This bread — you will not die.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for coming from heaven so that I don't have to fear death. You are life itself.
verses.discussion_question: Is it scary to think about dying? What does it mean to you that Jesus offers life that never ends?
verses.family_discussion: The great promise of the gospel is life over death. How should the fact that Jesus conquered death change the way our family thinks about hard and scary things?
verses.do_it_today: Read 1 Corinthians 15:55 — 'O death, where is your sting?' and thank Jesus for His victory.
verses.illustration_prompt: A glowing piece of bread descending from a bright heaven, and a person reaching up for it with an expression of wonder, the word 'LIFE' subtly woven into the light around them. Hopeful watercolour.

verses.kjv: I am the living bread which came down from heaven: if any man eat of this bread, he shall live for ever: and the bread that I will give is my flesh, which I will give for the life of the world.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I am the Living Bread from heaven. Whoever eats this bread will live forever. The bread I give is Myself — My life — which I will give so that the whole world can live.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'I am the living bread. Whoever receives Me will live forever — I give Myself for the world.'
verses.meaning: Jesus announces the cross before it happens. The bread He will give is His body — broken, given, poured out. This is the heart of the gospel in one sentence.
verses.memory_phrase: I give Myself for the life of the world.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for giving Yourself completely. I receive You today.
verses.discussion_question: If someone gave up their life so that others could live, what would you call that person?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said the bread is His flesh — He would give His body on the cross for the world. How do we respond to a gift that great?
verses.do_it_today: At your next meal, before you eat, quietly remember what Jesus gave — His own body — and say thank you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with His hands outstretched in a gesture of giving, light emanating from His heart outward toward the world, His expression full of love and sacrifice. The cross faintly visible in the light behind Him. Moving watercolour.

verses.kjv: The Jews therefore strove among themselves, saying, How can this man give us his flesh to eat?
verses.little_bible: The people began to argue among themselves: 'What does He mean — how can He give us His flesh to eat? That makes no sense!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: People argued, saying, 'What does He mean? How can He give us His flesh?'
verses.meaning: They took Jesus literally when He was speaking spiritually. The deepest truths of God often sound like nonsense to those who approach them only with human logic.
verses.memory_phrase: Spiritual truth goes deeper than human logic.
verses.prayer: Lord, when I don't understand something You say, help me keep seeking You with a humble heart.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever heard something that sounded strange at first, but later made sense?
verses.family_discussion: The crowd couldn't understand spiritual language through physical thinking. How do we grow in understanding spiritual truth? What habits help?
verses.do_it_today: If there's a Bible verse you don't understand, write it down and ask God to show you what it means.
verses.illustration_prompt: Small groups of people in debate, hands gesturing, expressions frustrated and confused, some pulling at each other's robes in argument, while Jesus stands calm in the background. Tense, honest watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except ye eat the flesh of the Son of man, and drink his blood, ye have no life in you.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told them: 'I tell you truly — unless you receive Me completely, trusting in everything I did for you, you do not have real life inside you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Unless you truly receive Me and trust in Me, you do not have life inside you.'
verses.meaning: Jesus intensifies rather than softens the challenge. To receive Jesus means receiving His sacrifice fully — His body broken, His blood poured out. This is total commitment, not partial acceptance.
verses.memory_phrase: Receive Jesus — completely, wholly.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I receive You fully — Your life, Your death, Your resurrection. All of You, in all of me.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to really receive a gift — not just saying thank you politely, but actually using it?
verses.family_discussion: To eat and drink Jesus means to fully depend on and receive His sacrifice. How do we help children understand this as real trust — not just religious words?
verses.do_it_today: Pray a prayer of complete reception: 'Lord Jesus, I receive You fully — not part of You, all of You.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking with great earnestness, both hands extended with palms up as if offering everything, His face intense with love, the crowd around Him stunned and still. Powerful, intimate watercolour.

verses.kjv: Whoso eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, hath eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Whoever truly receives Me — trusting in what I did — has eternal life, and I will raise them up on the last day.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Whoever truly trusts in Jesus and what He did has eternal life, and Jesus will raise them up on the last day.
verses.meaning: The promise is certain and repeated: receive Jesus, have eternal life, be raised at the last day. This is the gospel in miniature — present possession and future resurrection.
verses.memory_phrase: Receive Jesus — have eternal life, be raised up.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I trust in You and what You did for me. I believe in the resurrection — mine and Yours.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to be raised up on the last day? What do you picture when you hear that?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus promises both eternal life now AND resurrection at the last day. How should the hope of resurrection change the way we live every day?
verses.do_it_today: Ask a family member what they are looking forward to in God's eternal kingdom. Dream together about it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A joyful sunrise scene with a figure rising upward from darkness into brilliant light, arms raised in celebration, surrounded by golden radiance and blooming life. Hope and resurrection watercolour.

verses.kjv: For my flesh is meat indeed, and my blood is drink indeed.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'What I offer is truly food and truly drink — real nourishment for your soul, better than any meal you have ever had.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'I am truly what your soul needs — real food, real drink.'
verses.meaning: Jesus contrasts Himself with all earthly food. Every meal satisfies for a while; Jesus satisfies permanently and perfectly.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is truly what we need.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are what my soul truly needs. More than food, more than anything — I need You.
verses.discussion_question: What is your favourite food? Now imagine something that feeds your heart and soul — what would that be like?
verses.family_discussion: Our bodies need food every day. Our souls need Jesus every day. How do we help our family 'feed' on Jesus daily?
verses.do_it_today: Feed on Jesus today — read a bit of the Bible, pray, and spend time thinking about who He is.
verses.illustration_prompt: A beautifully set table with glowing bread and wine, but the light is coming not from the food but from Jesus who stands beside the table. The real nourishment is Him. Serene, warm watercolour.

verses.kjv: He that eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, dwelleth in me, and I in him.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Whoever truly receives Me lives in Me — and I live in them. We are together, inside each other!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Whoever receives Me lives in Me — and I live in them.'
verses.meaning: This is the mystery and miracle of union with Christ. Not merely relationship — but mutual indwelling. Jesus in us, us in Jesus. This is the deepest closeness possible.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus in me, me in Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, live in me. And let me live fully in You — every day, every moment.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean for two people to be really, truly close? Can you think of an example?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus says He lives in those who trust Him. How does the reality of Jesus living inside us change the way we make decisions or treat other people?
verses.do_it_today: Throughout the day today, remember: Jesus is in me. Let that change how you act and speak.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two glowing circles of light intertwining like a Venn diagram — one representing a human figure, one representing Jesus — their overlap glowing the brightest of all. Beautiful, abstract yet tender watercolour.

verses.kjv: As the living Father hath sent me, and I live by the Father: so he that eateth me, even he shall live by me.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'The living Father sent Me and I live through My connection to Him. In the same way, whoever receives Me will live through their connection to Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus lives through the Father. And whoever receives Jesus lives through Jesus.
verses.meaning: Life flows: from the Father to the Son, and from the Son to all who receive Him. We are connected to the very source of eternal life.
verses.memory_phrase: Life flows from Father to Son to us.
verses.prayer: Thank You, Father, for giving Jesus life. Thank You, Jesus, for sharing Your life with me.
verses.discussion_question: Think of water flowing through pipes to your house. How is that a bit like life flowing from God to Jesus to us?
verses.family_discussion: We 'live by' Jesus the way He 'lives by' the Father. What does it look like in daily life to actually depend on Jesus for everything?
verses.do_it_today: Before you start any task today, say: 'Jesus, I want to do this through You, not just by myself.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A glowing chain of life: the Father as a brilliant sun, rays flowing to Jesus, and from Jesus rays flowing outward to many people, all connected in a beautiful cascade of light and warmth. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: This is that bread which came down from heaven: not as your fathers did eat manna, and are dead: he that eateth of this bread shall live for ever.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'This is the bread from heaven — not like the manna your ancestors ate and then died anyway. Whoever receives this bread will live forever.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'This bread is different from manna. Whoever has this bread lives forever.'
verses.meaning: Jesus closes the bread-from-heaven argument with a final, clear contrast: manna was temporary; He is eternal. Old things foreshadow; He fulfils and surpasses them all.
verses.memory_phrase: This bread — live forever.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are the true bread. I choose You over every lesser thing.
verses.discussion_question: Which is better — something that helps you for a day or something that helps you forever? Why?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said He is better than manna — the greatest miracle of Moses. How does this help us understand Jesus' relationship to the whole Old Testament?
verses.do_it_today: Take a piece of bread at your next meal and let it remind you: Jesus is the true bread that gives eternal life.
verses.illustration_prompt: Split scene: left shows faded ancient manna and skeletal remains in a sandy desert; right shows a vibrant living garden with Jesus at the centre radiating life. Clear contrast, hopeful watercolour.

verses.kjv: These things said he in the synagogue, as he taught in Capernaum.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said all these things while teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum — the town where He often taught.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said all this while teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
verses.meaning: John anchors the great Bread of Life discourse in a specific, historic location. These weren't vague spiritual ideas — they were spoken in a real place, to real people.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus taught in the synagogue.
verses.prayer: Lord, thank You that Your truth is real — spoken in real places to real people, including me.
verses.discussion_question: Where do you go to learn about God? What makes that place special?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus taught in the synagogue — the community place of worship. How does gathering with other believers help us understand Jesus' teaching more deeply?
verses.do_it_today: If your family goes to church, pray a blessing over your church and the people who teach there.
verses.illustration_prompt: The interior of a first-century synagogue with stone pillars and simple wooden benches, Jesus teaching at the front, a diverse crowd listening with varied expressions in the warm lamplight. Historical watercolour.

verses.kjv: Many therefore of his disciples, when they had heard this, said, This is an hard saying; who can hear it?
verses.little_bible: Many of Jesus' followers, hearing all of this, said, 'This teaching is too hard — who could possibly accept it?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Many of Jesus' followers said, 'This teaching is too hard to accept!'
