verses.meaning: Some walked with Jesus but weren't truly committed. Hard teaching reveals the difference between fans and followers, between shallow interest and deep faith.
verses.memory_phrase: Hard teaching reveals true disciples.
verses.prayer: Lord, when Your teaching is hard, give me the grace to stay, to keep listening, and to trust You.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever found something really hard to understand or accept? What did you do?
verses.family_discussion: When Jesus' teaching is difficult, is it okay to wrestle with it? What is the difference between struggling honestly with hard truth and just walking away from it?
verses.do_it_today: Find one teaching of Jesus that is hard for you. Bring it to God in prayer and ask Him to help you receive it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of disciples with troubled, uncertain expressions, arms crossed, whispering to each other at the edge of a crowd. Some looking toward the exit. Jesus in the foreground teaching, unaware. Honest, searching watercolour.

verses.kjv: When Jesus knew in himself that his disciples murmured at it, he said unto them, Doth this offend you?
verses.little_bible: Jesus knew inside Himself that His followers were grumbling about His words. So He asked them directly: 'Does this offend you?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus knew they were complaining and asked them, 'Does this offend you?'
verses.meaning: Jesus knows our hidden reactions. He doesn't pretend not to notice when we pull back from His truth — He confronts it with a loving, direct question.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus knows our hidden reactions.
verses.prayer: Lord, You know every thought I have. Help me bring my doubts to You honestly rather than hiding them.
verses.discussion_question: Has anything Jesus taught ever surprised you or felt hard to accept?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus asked 'Does this offend you?' to the very people who called themselves His disciples. Is it possible to follow Jesus and still sometimes be offended by what He says?
verses.do_it_today: Bring one honest, hard question to Jesus in prayer today. He can handle it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus looking directly and gently at a group of shifting, uncomfortable disciples, His eyes full of understanding and compassion. His question hanging in the air between them. Intimate watercolour.

verses.kjv: What and if ye shall see the Son of man ascend up where he was before?
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked them: 'What will you think when you see the Son of Man go back up to where He was before?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked, 'What will you think when you see Me go back up to heaven where I was before?'
verses.meaning: Jesus points to His coming ascension — hinting that His origin in heaven is not a metaphor but a literal truth that will be visibly proven.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus came from heaven and returns to heaven.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You were in heaven before the world began. And You came for me. That is amazing.
verses.discussion_question: Where did Jesus come from before He was born as a baby? Where did He go after He rose from the dead?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus hints at His pre-existence and His ascension. How does knowing Jesus is eternal — before and after His time on earth — help us trust Him more fully?
verses.do_it_today: Read Acts 1:9-11 about Jesus' ascension. Imagine being there. What would you have felt?
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gesturing upward toward a bright opening in the clouds, light streaming down, the disciples looking up with awe and dawning wonder. The hint of heaven above. Glowing watercolour.

verses.kjv: It is the spirit that quickeneth; the flesh profiteth nothing: the words that I speak unto you, they are spirit, and they are life.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'It is the Spirit who gives life — the body alone can't do that. The words I speak to you are spiritual and alive — they carry real life!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'The Spirit gives life. My words are spirit and life — they are alive!'
verses.meaning: Jesus clarifies: His teaching about flesh and blood is spiritual, not physical. His words are not just information — they are alive and life-giving when received in faith.
verses.memory_phrase: My words are spirit and life.
verses.prayer: Lord, let Your words live in me. Let them not just sit in my head but change my heart and life.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between reading words on a page and feeling them really touch your heart?
verses.family_discussion: The Bible says God's Word is living and active (Hebrews 4:12). How does reading the Bible with the Holy Spirit's help feel different from just reading it like any other book?
verses.do_it_today: Read one verse from the Bible slowly, three times. Ask the Holy Spirit to bring it alive in your heart.
verses.illustration_prompt: An open Bible with words rising off the page as glowing wisps of golden light, drifting toward a figure who breathes them in. The Holy Spirit as a gentle dove hovering above. Vivid, spiritual watercolour.

verses.kjv: But there are some of you that believe not. For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were that believed not, and who should betray him.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'But some of you do not truly believe.' For Jesus knew from the very beginning who would not believe — and even who would betray Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Some of you do not truly believe.' He knew from the beginning who would trust Him and who would not.
verses.meaning: Nothing surprises Jesus — not doubt, not betrayal. He walked knowingly toward the cross, with full knowledge of who Judas was, and He loved him anyway.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus knew — and loved anyway.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You knew everything about me and You still came for me. Thank You for Your unstoppable love.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been let down by someone you trusted? How did that feel?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus knew Judas would betray Him and still chose him as a disciple. What does this tell us about the depth of God's patience and love, even for those who reject Him?
verses.do_it_today: Thank Jesus today for knowing every one of your failures — and still calling you to follow Him.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus looking across the disciples with all-knowing compassion, His gaze falling gently on one shadowed figure at the edge (Judas). Bittersweet, quiet, intimate watercolour. No anger — only knowing love.

verses.kjv: And he said, Therefore said I unto you, that no man can come unto me, except it were given unto him of my Father.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'That is why I told you — no one can come to Me unless the Father gives them the ability to do so.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'No one can come to Me unless the Father helps them to come.'
verses.meaning: Coming to Jesus is entirely a work of grace. This is not cause for despair — it is cause for prayer and trust that God's drawing work is real and powerful.
verses.memory_phrase: God makes it possible to come to Jesus.
verses.prayer: Father, thank You for making it possible for me to come to Jesus. I could not have come on my own.
verses.discussion_question: Has someone ever helped you reach something you couldn't get to on your own?
verses.family_discussion: If coming to Jesus is a gift from the Father, what does that mean about the way we pray for those who don't yet believe?
verses.do_it_today: Pray specifically for someone who seems far from Jesus: 'Father, draw them to Jesus. Give them the gift of faith.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child reaching upward toward Jesus, and above the child a loving hand (the Father's) gently helping lift them, creating the connection. Grace illustrated in movement. Beautiful watercolour.

verses.kjv: From that time many of his disciples went back, and walked no more with him.
verses.little_bible: After this, many of the people who had been following Jesus turned around and left — they stopped walking with Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: After this, many followers turned and left Jesus. They stopped following Him.
verses.meaning: This is one of the saddest verses in John. Many turned back — not enemies, but followers. Hard teaching and costly grace drove away those whose faith was shallow.
verses.memory_phrase: Many walked away. Will you?
verses.prayer: Lord, even when Your teaching is hard, give me the grace to stay close to You. I don't want to walk away.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever wanted to give up on something hard? What helped you keep going?
verses.family_discussion: What makes people walk away from Jesus? What helps people stay? How is our family strengthening their faith so they don't walk away when things get hard?
verses.do_it_today: Tell Jesus: 'Lord, I choose to stay. Even when it's hard — I'm not going anywhere.'
verses.illustration_prompt: People turning their backs and walking away down a road that leads into shadow, while a smaller group remains standing with Jesus who stands in warm light, His face sorrowful but loving. Poignant watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto the twelve, Will ye also go away?
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus turned to the twelve disciples and asked quietly: 'Will you also leave Me?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus turned to the twelve and asked, 'Will you also go away?'
verses.meaning: The most vulnerable question Jesus ever asked His inner circle. He does not chase those who leave — but He tenderly invites His closest friends to declare where they stand.
verses.memory_phrase: Will you also go away?
verses.prayer: Jesus, my answer is no. I am not leaving. I choose to stay with You — today and always.
verses.discussion_question: If Jesus asked you right now, 'Will you also go away?' — what would you say?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus asked the Twelve where they stood after many walked away. When our faith is tested or a wave of doubt comes, how does our family stay anchored to Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Have each family member answer Jesus' question out loud: 'No, Lord. I will not go away.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus turning to face twelve disciples, His expression gentle and searching, the road still visible where others have walked away. The twelve standing steady, waiting to answer. A defining moment in watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then Simon Peter answered him, Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast the words of eternal life.
verses.little_bible: Simon Peter answered: 'Lord, where else would we go? You have the words that give eternal life!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Peter said, 'Lord, where else would we go? You have the words of eternal life!'
verses.meaning: Peter's confession is one of the greatest in all of Scripture — not 'we stay because it's comfortable' but 'there is nowhere else to go.' Jesus is not one option among many. He is the only One.
verses.memory_phrase: Lord, where else would we go?
verses.prayer: Lord, You have the words of eternal life. There is nowhere else I would rather be than with You.
verses.discussion_question: If you had to leave everything else behind, what one person or thing would you never want to leave?
verses.family_discussion: Peter didn't say following Jesus was easy — he said there was nowhere else to go. How does that honesty make his faith feel real? Can we say the same?
verses.do_it_today: Memorise Peter's words together: 'Lord, to whom shall we go? You have the words of eternal life.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Peter stepping forward with one hand on his heart and his face earnest and passionate, his eyes locked on Jesus. The eleven standing firm behind him. The empty road where others left visible in the background. Powerful watercolour.

verses.kjv: And we believe and are sure that thou art that Christ, the Son of the living God.
verses.little_bible: Peter continued: 'And we believe — we are completely sure — that You are the Christ, the Son of the living God!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Peter said, 'We are sure — You are the Christ, the Son of the living God!'
verses.meaning: Peter's great confession before the cross. Belief plus certainty — not presumption but hard-won confidence. 'The living God' sets Jesus apart from every idol: the one true God who is alive.
verses.memory_phrase: You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe and I am sure — You are the Christ, the Son of the living God. My Lord and my Saviour.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between hoping something is true and being sure about it?
verses.family_discussion: Peter said 'we believe AND we are sure.' Certainty in faith doesn't come from our feelings — where does it come from? How does your family build confidence in who Jesus is?
verses.do_it_today: Say Peter's confession out loud as your own: 'I believe and I am sure — You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Peter with both hands on his heart, his face radiant with conviction, eyes full of love and faith, looking at Jesus who receives the confession with a warm, affirming smile. The eleven behind Peter equally resolute. Triumphant watercolour.

verses.kjv: Jesus answered them, Have not I chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil?
verses.little_bible: Jesus replied: 'Did I not choose you twelve Myself? And yet one of you is working for the enemy.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'I chose all twelve of you — but one of you belongs to the enemy.'
verses.meaning: Even inside the circle of the chosen, there is one who will betray. Jesus knew — and yet He bore that knowledge throughout His ministry. The cross was never a surprise to Him.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus knew — and kept going.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You carried so much. Even betrayal from inside Your circle. Help me never take Your suffering lightly.
verses.discussion_question: How would it feel to know that someone in your group of friends would one day let you down badly?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus knew about Judas from the beginning yet treated him with dignity throughout. What can we learn from that about how to treat people, even those who may hurt us?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for someone who has hurt you. Ask God to help you love them as Jesus loved Judas — with honesty and grace.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus looking at all twelve disciples together, His gaze serene yet carrying a trace of sorrow. One figure stands slightly apart in shadow, his expression hidden. The weight of foreknowledge shown quietly in Jesus' eyes. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: He spake of Judas Iscariot the son of Simon: for he it was that should betray him, being one of the twelve.
verses.little_bible: John tells us Jesus was talking about Judas Iscariot, the son of Simon. He was the one who would betray Jesus — and he was one of the twelve disciples.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus was talking about Judas Iscariot. He was one of the twelve, but he would betray Jesus.
verses.meaning: John names Judas plainly — not to shock us, but to show us that the story of Jesus includes betrayal, and Jesus walked into it with open eyes and open arms. He was not a victim of circumstance but a willing sacrifice.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus walked knowingly to the cross.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You knew what was coming and You still came. You still chose the cross for me. Thank You.
verses.discussion_question: If you knew something hard was coming, would you keep going? What gave Jesus the strength to keep going?
verses.family_discussion: Judas was one of the twelve — someone who walked closely with Jesus. This shows that closeness to Jesus doesn't automatically mean we belong to Him. What makes someone truly belong to Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Ask yourself: am I close to Jesus in my head only, or am I truly His in my heart? Talk to Him about it.
verses.illustration_prompt: John's Gospel scroll with the name 'Judas Iscariot' inscribed in careful letters, a single shadow falling over the name, while in the background a table of twelve is set with light and bread. The contrast of belonging and betrayal. Thoughtful watercolour.
